perception_themes: AI frames 'luxury EV' as synonymous with Tesla Model S and Model X across editorial and consumer sources
BMW i4 and iX appear in dedicated reviews but are not retrieved as default answers for luxury EV queries
missing_associations: BMW i4 and iX as serious luxury EV contenders with driving dynamics heritage carried into electric
BMW's Neue Klasse platform as next-generation electric architecture rivaling Tesla's engineering
findings.competitor_association: Tesla dominates luxury EV discourse across Electrek, InsideEVs, Car and Driver, and consumer forums as the default reference point for premium electric vehicles.
findings.safe_scenario_wording: In Peec data, BMW holds 12% visibility at position 2.8 in Luxury EV Transition; Tavily shows luxury EV queries resolve to Tesla Model S/X coverage rather than BMW i-series.
findings.rationale: Tavily target sources return BMW EV content in model-specific reviews but not in category-level luxury EV queries, while competitor sources frame Tesla as the luxury EV default — consistent with a perception gap where product exists but narrative association lags.

perception_themes: AI defaults to Mercedes GLE and GLC when answering premium SUV queries across editorial and review sources
BMW X3 and X5 appear in comparison articles but are not the primary retrieval result for the category
missing_associations: BMW X3/X5 as segment-leading premium SUVs with best-in-class driving dynamics
BMW X-series sales leadership in key markets as evidence of segment relevance
findings.competitor_association: Mercedes-Benz GLE and GLC are the default premium SUV references in MotorTrend, Car and Driver, and Edmunds retrieval results for category-level queries.
findings.safe_scenario_wording: In Peec data, BMW holds 15% visibility at position 2.5 in Premium SUV Segment; Tavily shows premium SUV queries surface Mercedes GLE/GLC content ahead of BMW X-series.
findings.rationale: Tavily target sources confirm BMW X-series content exists and is well-reviewed, but category-level retrieval favors Mercedes models — consistent with an indexing gap where content is present but not surfacing in AI retrieval.

perception_themes: AI associates electric vehicle coverage with Tesla Model 3 and Model Y due to overwhelming editorial volume
BMW i4 and iX reviews exist but are outnumbered by Tesla coverage by an order of magnitude
missing_associations: BMW i4 as a driver's EV with M-Sport credentials and rear-wheel-drive character
BMW iX as a technology flagship with next-generation iDrive and sustainable materials
findings.competitor_association: Tesla Model 3 and Model Y dominate electric vehicle editorial volume across Electrek, InsideEVs, CNET, and mainstream press with multi-year coverage depth.
findings.safe_scenario_wording: In Peec data, BMW holds 6% visibility at position 3.5 in Electric i-Series despite active products; Tavily shows electric vehicle queries surface Tesla models almost exclusively due to content volume advantage.
findings.rationale: Tavily target sources confirm BMW i4 and iX reviews exist, but Peec 6% visibility indicates content volume is insufficient to compete with Tesla's editorial moat — consistent with a volume/frequency gap where coverage depth prevents surfacing.

perception_themes: AI increasingly associates 'luxury automotive heritage' with Mercedes-Benz over BMW in historical and brand-narrative queries
BMW's 'Ultimate Driving Machine' heritage is recognized but narrowed to performance rather than luxury lineage
missing_associations: BMW's motorsport heritage (M division, touring car championships) as luxury brand credential
BMW's design evolution from the 2002 to modern lineup as a continuous luxury narrative
findings.competitor_association: Mercedes-Benz owns luxury heritage association across Wikipedia, brand history articles, and automotive journalism with references to the invention of the automobile and Silver Arrows racing lineage.
findings.safe_scenario_wording: In Peec data, BMW holds 30% visibility at position 1.8 in Brand Heritage but Mercedes-Benz is the competitor owner; Tavily shows luxury heritage queries increasingly favor Mercedes founding mythology over BMW's performance lineage.
findings.rationale: Tavily target sources frame BMW heritage through the performance lens (M cars, driving dynamics) but not as luxury heritage, while competitor sources connect Mercedes to the broader luxury-automotive origin story — consistent with a perception gap where heritage exists but is categorized differently by AI.
